enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error, crash, or refuse to emit) a SAM record where sum of lengths of M/I/S/=/X CIGAR operations does not equal SEQ length

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.
Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a SAM record where FLAG bit 0x4 (segment unmapped) is set but RNAME != '*' and POS > 0, violating the mapped/unmapped contradiction rule

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.
Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject paired reads where both ends have same-signed TLEN values, violating the opposite-signed pairing rule for template ends

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

enforced.mr_name: Optional tag type character violation rejection
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a SAM record with an optional tag using an illegal type character outside AifZHB

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields. Tags containing lowercase letters are reserved for local use and will not be formally defined in any future version of this specification.

As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields. Tags containing lowercase letters are reserved for local use and will not be formally defined in any future version of this specification.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header TAG:VALUE pair ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: The parsed SAM header should be semantically equivalent after shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within @HD line

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: The parsed SAM alignment records should be semantically equivalent after shuffling optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @SQ record TAG:VALUE pair ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: The parsed SAM header should be semantically equivalent after shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within each @SQ line

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @PG record TAG:VALUE pair ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: The parsed SAM header should be semantically equivalent after shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within each @PG line

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: The parsed alignment's reference coordinates, query-consumed length, and alignment semantics must be identical after splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operators (e.g., 10M ↔ 4M6M).

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' (ADVISORY) rather than 'must', indicating that while adjacent identical operators are discouraged, they are not prohibited and parsers should treat them equivalently.

quarantine.mr_name: Coordinate boundary invariance under SAM-BAM roundtrip
quarantine.oracle: POS coordinate values (1-based) must remain identical after SAM→BAM→SAM conversion; getAlignmentStart() and getAlignmentEnd() must return same values

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: * If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: * If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

quarantine.mr_name: 1-based coordinate query invariance under optional tag permutation
quarantine.oracle: Coordinate query methods must return identical 1-based values after shuffling optional tag fields

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p145
evidence.quote: As usual POS should be between 1 and the @SQ header's LN value, but POS plus the sum of the lengths of M=XDN CIGAR operations may exceed LN. Coordinates greater than LN are interpreted by subtracting LN so that bases at +1, +2, +3, … are considered to be mapped at positions 1,2,3,…; thus each (1-based) position p is interpreted as ((p-1))+1.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

quarantine.mr_name: Coordinate boundary preservation under header subtag shuffling
quarantine.oracle: LN (reference length) values in @SQ headers must remain identical after shuffling subtags and roundtrip; coordinate boundaries defined by LN must be preserved

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: * If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

quarantine.oracle: parse(x) must deep-equal parse(T(x)) after shuffling header field order and writer round-trip

quarantine.oracle: parse(x) must deep-equal parse(T(x)) after splitting/merging adjacent identical CIGAR operators and writer round-trip

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header field tags permutation invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsed header must be semantically equivalent after shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within each @SQ, @RG, and @PG line

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Coordinate query invariance under header reordering
quarantine.oracle: Coordinate-related query method results must remain identical when @SQ header records are reordered

evidence.quote: A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is $[3,7]$. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

quarantine.oracle: parse(seed) must equal parse(sut_write_roundtrip(seed)) after canonical normalization

Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than
once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once
and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.
